Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Body: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Decision: Maintained status quo on repo rate.
Reason: Easing inflation concerns, focus on growth.
Factors influencing future decisions: Inflation trajectory, global economic indicators, domestic demand.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and instruments of the RBI's monetary policy, particularly in the context of managing inflation and fostering economic growth.
Analyze the factors influencing the Monetary Policy Committee's decision to maintain the repo rate, considering both domestic and global economic conditions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy in addressing supply-side inflation and its interaction with fiscal policy measures.
Examine the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the imperative of supporting economic recovery and financial stability.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.

GS3.headline: Latest Wildlife Census Reveals Significant Increase in Population of Key Species Across India
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change released the latest nationwide wildlife census, showing a robust increase in populations of several key species.
• Notable increases were observed in the numbers of tigers, elephants, one-horned rhinoceros, and various avian species, indicating successful conservation efforts.
• The census employed advanced methodologies, including camera traps, DNA analysis, and citizen science inputs, to ensure accuracy.
• This positive trend is attributed to habitat restoration projects, anti-poaching measures, community involvement, and dedicated wildlife protection laws.
• The findings reinforce India's commitment to biodiversity conservation and its status as a mega-diverse country.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Bio-diversity and Environment.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Released by: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Key finding: Significant increase in populations of tigers, elephants, one-horned rhinos, avian species.
Methodology: Camera traps, DNA analysis, citizen science.
Reasons for increase: Habitat restoration, anti-poaching, community involvement, protection laws.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the latest wildlife census findings in assessing the effectiveness of India's biodiversity conservation strategies.
Analyze the key factors and policies, such as Project Tiger and habitat protection, that have contributed to the increase in populations of endangered species.
Examine the role of community participation and advanced scientific methodologies in successful wildlife conservation efforts in India.
Evaluate the challenges that still persist in wildlife protection, including human-wildlife conflict, habitat fragmentation, and climate change impacts.
GS3.imageDescription: A tiger in its natural habitat, symbolizing successful conservation.
GS3.trivia: India is home to over 70% of the world's tiger population.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Foreign Trade Policy with Focus on Export Boost and Digitalization
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry launched a new Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) aimed at boosting India's exports to reach a target of USD 2 trillion by 2030.
• The policy emphasizes digitalization of trade processes, e-commerce exports, and district-level export hubs to integrate MSMEs into global supply chains.
• New schemes and incentives have been introduced to support emerging sectors and facilitate ease of doing business for exporters.
• It also focuses on promoting Brand India, diversifying export markets, and addressing non-tariff barriers faced by Indian products abroad.
• The FTP is designed to be dynamic and responsive to global trade dynamics, with provisions for periodic reviews and adjustments.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Policy: New Foreign Trade Policy (FTP).
Launch by: Ministry of Commerce and Industry.
Target: USD 2 trillion exports by 2030.
Key focus areas: Digitalization, e-commerce exports, district-level export hubs, MSME integration, Brand India.
Nature: Dynamic, with periodic reviews.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and key features of India's new Foreign Trade Policy and its potential to significantly boost the country's export performance.
Analyze how the policy's emphasis on digitalization, e-commerce, and district-level hubs can integrate MSMEs into global trade and foster inclusive growth.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in diversifying its export basket and markets, especially in the context of evolving global supply chains.
Examine the role of government incentives and ease of doing business reforms in enhancing the competitiveness of Indian exports.
GS3.imageDescription: Container ships at a bustling port, symbolizing international trade.
GS3.trivia: India's merchandise exports crossed the USD 400 billion mark for the first time in FY22.

GS3.headline: India Announces Ambitious New Target for Renewable Energy Capacity by 2035
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced an ambitious new target to achieve 700 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2035.
• This revised target significantly upgrades previous goals, reflecting India's accelerated commitment to clean energy transition and climate action.
• The plan includes massive investments in solar, wind, hydro, and green hydrogen projects, alongside grid modernization and energy storage solutions.
• The move is expected to attract substantial domestic and foreign investment, creating a robust green economy and numerous jobs.
• Achieving this target will position India as a global leader in renewable energy and substantially reduce its carbon footprint.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Announcing body: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy.
New target: 700 GW renewable energy capacity by 2035.
Scope: Solar, wind, hydro, green hydrogen, grid modernization, energy storage.
Expected impact: Attract investment, create jobs, reduce carbon footprint, global leadership in RE.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's new ambitious renewable energy target for its climate action goals and energy security.
Analyze the strategies and investments required to achieve 700 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2035, including technological advancements and policy support.
Examine the economic and environmental benefits of a rapid transition to renewable energy, including job creation and reduced dependence on fossil fuels.
Evaluate the challenges in integrating such a large quantum of intermittent renewable energy into the national grid and the role of energy storage solutions.
GS3.imageDescription: A vast solar power farm stretching towards the horizon.
GS3.trivia: India's current installed renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) is over 170 GW.
